title: 10 possible side effects of Crocus sativa (Saffron) if used improperly
intro: Crocus sativa, also known as saffron, can have potentially harmful side effects if used improperly. Overconsumption of saffron can lead to poisoning, demonstrating symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness. In severe cases, it can cause miscarriage, blindness, and even death. Additionally, saffron may interact negatively with certain medications, leading to adverse reactions. Furthermore, using saffron in amounts far greater than the recommended dosage may result in poisoning, making it crucial to adhere to recommended usage guidelines. Long-term use of large amounts of saffron can also result in yellowing of the skin, eyes, and mucous membranes, a condition known as safraninism. Therefore, it is important to use saffron responsibly and seek medical advice before incorporating it into any medicinal regimen.
